title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
authors: Patrick Lewis; Ethan Perez; Aleksandra Piktus; Fabio Petroni; Vladimir Karpukhin; Naman Goyal; Heinrich Küttler; Mike Lewis; Wen-tau Yih; Tim Rocktäschel; Sebastian Riedel; Douwe Kiela
method: RAG with parametric memory and non-parametric memory using dense retrieval over Wikipedia
dataset: Open-domain QA and knowledge-intensive NLP tasks
metrics: Retrieval and generation performance across QA and generation tasks
limitations: Original RAG formulation focuses on knowledge-intensive NLP but not specifically on academic literature-review citation verification
notes: Foundational paper for understanding classical RAG architecture

title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Shahul Es; Jithin James; Luis Espinosa-Anke; Steven Schockaert
method: Reference-free evaluation framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines
dataset: RAG pipelines and retrieval-generation evaluation settings
metrics: Faithfulness; answer relevancy; context precision; context recall
limitations: Evaluation-focused work but not a complete global academic literature-review RAG system
notes: Important paper for designing evaluation metrics in ScholarRAG-Eval

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
authors: Yunfan Gao; Yun Xiong; Xinyu Gao; Kangxiang Jia; Jinliu Pan; Yuxi Bi; Yi Dai; Jiawei Sun; Meng Wang; Haofen Wang
method: Survey of Naive RAG Advanced RAG and Modular RAG paradigms
dataset: RAG systems; retrieval; generation; augmentation; evaluation frameworks
metrics: RAG taxonomy; system components; evaluation discussion; research challenges
limitations: Survey paper provides broad taxonomy but is not an implementation or experiment-specific benchmark for this project
notes: Useful for literature review structure and terminology

title: RAGChecker: A Fine-grained Framework for Diagnosing Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Dongyu Ru; Lin Qiu; Xiangkun Hu; Tianhang Zhang; Peng Shi; Shuaichen Chang; Cheng Jiayang; Cunxiang Wang; Shichao Sun; Huanyu Li; Zizao Zhang; Binjie Wang; Jiarong Jiang; Tong He; Zhiguo Wang; Pengfei Liu; Yue Zhang; Zheng Zhang
method: Fine-grained diagnostic evaluation framework for RAG retrieval and generation modules
dataset: 8 RAG systems evaluated across benchmark settings
metrics: Retriever and generator diagnostic metrics; correlation with human judgments
limitations: Focused on evaluation diagnostics, not specifically on academic literature-review RAG or citation verification
notes: Useful for designing fine-grained evaluation modules in ScholarRAG-Eval

title: RAGBench: Explainable Benchmark for Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
authors: Robert Friel; Masha Belyi; Atindriyo Sanyal
method: Large-scale explainable benchmark and TRACe framework for evaluating RAG systems
dataset: 100k RAG examples across five industry-specific domains and multiple RAG task types
metrics: TRACe evaluation metrics; benchmark comparisons with LLM-based evaluators and finetuned RoBERTa
limitations: Industry-focused benchmark; not designed specifically for academic literature-review reasoning
notes: Useful for benchmark design and explainable RAG evaluation

title: Enabling Large Language Models to Generate Text with Citations
authors: Tianyu Gao; Howard Yen; Jiatong Yu; Danqi Chen
method: ALCE benchmark for evaluating LLM-generated text with citations
dataset: ASQA; QAMPARI; ELI5 with retrieval corpora
limitations: Focuses on citation-grounded generation, not full academic paper corpus-level RAG
notes: Directly useful for citation verification and unsupported-claim detection

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
authors: Akari Asai; Zeqiu Wu; Yizhong Wang; Avirup Sil; Hannaneh Hajishirzi
method: Self-reflective RAG framework where the model learns when to retrieve, generate, and critique using reflection tokens
dataset: Open-domain QA; reasoning; fact verification; long-form generation tasks
limitations: Requires training or specialized model behavior, so it is not a simple plug-and-play pipeline for beginner implementation
notes: Useful for understanding adaptive retrieval and self-checking generation

title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Shi-Qi Yan; Jia-Chen Gu; Yun Zhu; Zhen-Hua Ling
method: CRAG uses a lightweight retrieval evaluator, web-search extension, and decompose-then-recompose strategy to improve generation when retrieval quality is poor
dataset: Four datasets covering short-form and long-form generation tasks
metrics: Generation performance across short-form and long-form tasks
limitations: Depends on retrieval-evaluator quality and may add complexity through web-search extension
notes: Useful for handling weak or misleading retrieved evidence

title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
authors: Darren Edge; Ha Trinh; Newman Cheng; Joshua Bradley; Alex Chao; Apurva Mody; Steven Truitt; Dasha Metropolitansky; Robert Osazuwa Ness; Jonathan Larson
method: GraphRAG builds an entity graph and community summaries to answer global questions over large private corpora
dataset: Datasets around the 1 million token range for global sensemaking questions
metrics: Comprehensiveness; diversity; comparison against conventional RAG baseline
limitations: More complex than standard RAG and may require graph construction and summary generation overhead
notes: Directly relevant to corpus-level academic literature-review questions

title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
authors: Nelson F. Liu; Kevin Lin; John Hewitt; Ashwin Paranjape; Michele Bevilacqua; Fabio Petroni; Percy Liang
method: Evaluation of how language models use long input contexts when relevant information appears at different positions
metrics: Accuracy under different relevant-information positions in context
limitations: Not a RAG system itself, but explains a key weakness of relying only on long context
notes: Useful justification for retrieval, reranking, and context-selection experiments
